Name: Roland-Garros
Tagline: The exclusive home of Roland-Garros tennis delivering live scores, schedules, draws, players, news, photos, videos and the most complete coverage of The 2026 Roland-Garros Tournament.
About: Amélie Mauresmo, Tournament Director, and Gilles Moretton, President of the French Tennis Federation, unveiled the new features of Roland‑Garros 2026 on Thursday, during the eagerly awaited pre‑tournament press conference. From enhanced player services and performance innovations to tributes celebrating legendary champions, an ever more ambitious Opening Week and a wide range of events both inside and outside the stadium, here is an overview of what’s new at the Parisian Grand Slam in 2026.
Philosophy: For the first time this year, players competing across the 24 events (899 matches scheduled) at Roland‑Garros will be allowed to use connected devices to collect biometric data.
Outcomes: Hampered by a right wrist injury sustained in recent weeks, the Murcia native chose not to take any further risks. A few days ago, during the Laureus World Sports Awards, the world No.1 had already casted doubt over his participation in the season’s second Grand Slam.
Admissions: To attend Roland-Garros, purchase tickets through the official website during the designated sales period. Early registration is recommended due to high demand, with various ticket packages available to suit different preferences and budgets.
Quote: Embrace the spirit of competition and unity, where tradition meets innovation at the heart of tennis excellence.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: Roland-Garros employs certified coaches with ATP and WTA experience, ensuring high-level expertise in player development. Coaches specialize in biomechanics and sports psychology, providing comprehensive training tailored to individual athlete needs.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: Roland-Garros offers on-site physiotherapy and sports psychology services to support player wellbeing. A dedicated team provides personalized care, focusing on injury prevention and mental resilience during the tournament.
Performance.Academic Integration: Roland-Garros collaborates with educational institutions to offer online learning for athletes balancing sport and academics. Tailored programs ensure players maintain academic progress alongside rigorous training schedules.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Roland-Garros champions have historically advanced to top ATP and WTA rankings, with many securing Grand Slam titles. The tournament serves as a critical platform for emerging talent to gain international recognition.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The Roland-Garros complex features 20 clay courts, including the iconic Philippe-Chatrier Stadium with a retractable roof. State-of-the-art training facilities and player lounges enhance the tournament experience.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Roland-Garros implements advanced analytics to monitor player performance and provide real-time feedback. Regular assessments and strategy sessions ensure continuous improvement and competitive edge.
